governance.entry_policy: Every active, retained, weakened, split, rejected, or superseded hypothesis must have a permanent record.
governance.immutability_policy: Hypothesis records are not deleted. Status changes and revisions are appended to history.
governance.evidence_policy: Supporting and challenging evidence must reference permanent Evidence IDs.
governance.confidence_policy: Confidence labels are qualitative unless a separately governed quantitative model is adopted.
governance.canonical_source_policy: This JSON file is the canonical machine-readable hypothesis registry. Narrative hypothesis documents may supplement but must not contradict it.

hypotheses.title: Framework Engineering
hypotheses.statement: Frameworks can be studied as engineered knowledge artifacts with identifiable structure, constraints, operations, and measurable properties.
alternative_explanations: Observed regularities may reflect common writing conventions rather than engineering-like structure.
The construct may be useful descriptively without constituting a distinct engineering discipline.
kill_conditions: Repeated studies fail to identify stable, reproducible structural properties across frameworks.
Proposed measurements do not outperform ordinary qualitative description.
hypotheses.next_experiment: Independent replication using a blinded external corpus.

review.rationale: The overall research program remains plausible, but the discipline-level claim is not yet validated.

hypotheses.title: Representation Independence
hypotheses.statement: At least some framework structure remains recoverable across materially different representations of the same underlying procedure.
alternative_explanations: Models may rely on semantic familiarity or memorized framework patterns.
Normalization may artificially increase apparent structural agreement.
kill_conditions: Structural recovery falls to chance or lexical-baseline levels under unfamiliar or adversarial representations.
Independent evaluators cannot reproduce cross-representation agreement.
hypotheses.next_experiment: Separate structural recovery from semantic familiarity using novel or synthetic frameworks.

review.rationale: Evidence is consistent with representation-independent structure, but semantic familiarity has not been isolated.

hypotheses.title: Framework Composition
hypotheses.statement: Frameworks can be decomposed into reusable structural components and composed from lower-level cognitive and procedural elements.
alternative_explanations: Apparent components may be analyst-imposed abstractions.
Decomposition may vary too much across evaluators to support a stable composition model.
kill_conditions: Independent decompositions show persistently low agreement.
Proposed components fail to recombine into recognizable procedural behavior.
hypotheses.next_experiment: Independent decomposition and recomposition study across multiple framework families.

review.rationale: Composition remains plausible but is less directly tested than representation independence or structural recognition.

hypotheses.title: Primitive Cognitive Operations
hypotheses.statement: Framework procedures can be described using a bounded or extensible taxonomy of primitive cognitive operations.
alternative_explanations: Primitive operations may be artifacts of the chosen coding scheme.
The operation set may be open-ended and unsuitable as a stable taxonomy.
kill_conditions: No coding scheme yields acceptable inter-rater reliability.
The primitive set grows without convergence as corpus size increases.
hypotheses.next_experiment: Taxonomy convergence and inter-rater reliability study.

review.rationale: Retained as an active foundational hypothesis pending direct testing.

hypotheses.title: Predictive Framework Engineering
hypotheses.statement: Measured structural properties of frameworks can eventually predict aspects of framework behavior, usability, transfer, failure, or fit.
alternative_explanations: Framework outcomes may be dominated by context, facilitation, or user skill.
Structural measures may describe artifacts without predicting real-world behavior.
kill_conditions: Structural measures repeatedly fail to predict predefined outcomes above baseline.
Predictive effects disappear under external validation.
hypotheses.next_experiment: Define one narrow behavioral outcome and preregister a predictive test.

review.rationale: No current evidence supports a predictive claim.

hypotheses.title: Primitive Grammar
hypotheses.statement: Primitive cognitive operations can be related through a grammar that constrains valid or meaningful framework procedures.
alternative_explanations: Observed sequencing may be domain-specific rather than grammatical.
A graph or workflow model may be sufficient without a grammar.
kill_conditions: No stable sequencing or composition constraints emerge across frameworks.
A simpler non-grammatical model explains the data equally well.
hypotheses.next_experiment: Compare grammar-based and graph-only models on held-out framework procedures.

review.rationale: Remains a theoretical extension requiring direct evidence.

hypotheses.title: Artifact Taxonomy
hypotheses.statement: Knowledge artifacts can be classified into reproducible types based on structural and procedural properties.
alternative_explanations: Artifact categories may be conventional labels rather than natural structural classes.
Hybrid artifacts may prevent stable classification.
kill_conditions: Independent classification produces persistently low agreement.
Proposed categories do not improve explanation, measurement, or prediction.
hypotheses.next_experiment: Blind classification study using the FE-008 reference corpus.

review.rationale: Active but not yet directly supported by accepted evidence.

hypotheses.title: Artifact Boundaries
hypotheses.statement: Operational boundaries between frameworks and adjacent artifact types can be defined and applied consistently.
alternative_explanations: Boundaries may be inherently fuzzy and context-dependent.
Prototype-based classification may be more appropriate than necessary-and-sufficient criteria.
kill_conditions: Boundary rules cannot achieve acceptable inter-rater agreement.
Boundary distinctions provide no analytical benefit.
hypotheses.next_experiment: Boundary-case adjudication study with mixed artifact types.

review.rationale: Active foundational hypothesis pending direct boundary testing.

hypotheses.title: Identity-Capability Principle
hypotheses.statement: An artifact's identity should be distinguished from the capabilities it enables or supports.
alternative_explanations: Artifact identity may be partly constituted by intended capability.
The distinction may be analytically useful but not empirically discrete.
kill_conditions: Independent evaluators cannot distinguish identity claims from capability claims.
The distinction does not improve classification or boundary decisions.
hypotheses.next_experiment: Evaluate identity-versus-capability judgments across ambiguous artifacts.

review.rationale: Active refinement of the artifact-boundary hypothesis.

hypotheses.title: Recognition Bias
hypotheses.statement: Recognition or familiarity with a named framework can influence procedural extraction and structural interpretation.
alternative_explanations: Differences attributed to recognition may instead reflect artifact clarity or corpus quality.
Recognition may improve accuracy rather than introduce bias.
kill_conditions: Blinded and recognized conditions produce no meaningful differences across adequately powered studies.
Observed differences are fully explained by artifact quality.

review.rationale: Recognition remains a credible threat to validity and must be experimentally isolated.

hypotheses.title: Measurement Reproducibility
hypotheses.statement: A frozen comparator, canonical normalization, and complete audit trail can produce reproducible Framework Engineering measurements.
alternative_explanations: Reproducibility may reflect deterministic tooling rather than valid measurement.
Results may not reproduce across operators, environments, or corpora.
kill_conditions: Identical inputs and versions do not produce identical outputs.
Independent operators cannot reproduce outputs from the audit record.
Material results depend on undocumented manual intervention.
hypotheses.next_experiment: Independent operator replication using the frozen comparator and archived packet.

review.rationale: This is currently among the strongest-supported hypotheses because it concerns the research instrument rather than the broader theory.

hypotheses.title: Structural Recognition
hypotheses.statement: Procedural structure can be recovered from framework artifacts with meaningful consistency beyond surface wording alone.
alternative_explanations: Models may infer expected structure from semantic familiarity.
Comparator assumptions may favor the target decomposition.
kill_conditions: Recovery does not exceed lexical or chance baselines under blinded unfamiliar conditions.
Independent evaluators fail to reproduce the structural result.
hypotheses.next_experiment: Use synthetic, renamed, or novel artifacts to isolate structure from recognition.

review.rationale: The phenomenon appears present, but competing explanations have not yet been isolated.

hypotheses.title: Procedural Compression
hypotheses.statement: Frameworks compress larger sets of procedural judgments into smaller reusable representations.
alternative_explanations: Apparent compression may simply be abstraction or summarization.
Compression may not preserve enough information to support reliable execution.
kill_conditions: Expanded procedures cannot be reconstructed with useful fidelity.
Compression measures fail to distinguish frameworks from ordinary summaries.
hypotheses.next_experiment: Measure reconstruction fidelity from compressed and non-compressed procedural artifacts.

review.rationale: Evidence is suggestive but the construct needs a formal operational definition.

hypotheses.title: Procedural Isomorphism
hypotheses.statement: Different frameworks may share equivalent or near-equivalent procedural structures despite differing domains, terminology, or presentation.
alternative_explanations: Similarities may result from coarse coding.
Common procedural motifs may be too generic to establish meaningful isomorphism.
kill_conditions: Similarity disappears under finer-grained constraint analysis.
Matched structures do not survive independent coding or negative controls.
hypotheses.next_experiment: Run preregistered positive and negative control comparisons across domains.

review.rationale: Cross-domain similarities were observed, but stronger controls are required.
